evals.prompt: ok I think I'm done implementing the new parser module, ready to commit
evals.expected_output: Detects .pre-commit-config.yaml, runs 'pre-commit run --all-files', shows output clearly, tells user whether they're clear to commit or not. Does NOT make the commit.

assertions.text: Response checks for .pre-commit-config.yaml (e.g. runs git rev-parse or checks the file path)
Response includes actual pre-commit command output (not just a description of what it would do)
Response clearly tells the user whether checks passed or failed
Response does NOT attempt to run 'git commit' or stage files

evals.prompt: I'm done with my changes, let's commit
evals.expected_output: Detects no .pre-commit-config.yaml, tells user pre-commit is not configured, stops gracefully. Does NOT run pre-commit or commit.

assertions.text: Response identifies that .pre-commit-config.yaml is not present and tells the user
Response does NOT attempt to run 'git commit' or stage files

evals.expected_output: Explicit invocation: detects .pre-commit-config.yaml, runs 'pre-commit run --all-files', shows full output, reports clear pass/fail. Does NOT commit.

assertions.text: Response includes actual pre-commit command output
Response clearly reports pass or fail status
Response does NOT attempt to run 'git commit'
